message:
fix(build): escape tab literal in rename pairs and isolate per-target artifact dirs

Two small but important fixes for multi-target artifact collection:

1. dsr: Replace "\t" with $'\t' in artifact_rename_pairs array
   entries. The \t inside double quotes produces a literal backslash-t
   rather than a tab character, which broke the jq-based manifest
   patching that splits on tabs to find old→new name mappings.

2. act_runner: Append /${platform//\//-} to the artifact directory
   path so each target's artifacts land in a separate subdirectory
   (e.g., build-tool-12345/linux-amd64/). Previously all targets
   wrote into the same flat directory, causing name collisions when
   multiple platforms produce identically-named binaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1969,7 +1969,7 @@ EOF
                 log_warn "Artifact name collision: $name (target=$target); saving as $renamed"
                 name="$renamed"
                 dest="$output_dir/$name"
-                artifact_rename_pairs+=("${target}||${orig_name}\t${name}")
+                artifact_rename_pairs+=("${target}||${orig_name}"$'\t'"${name}")
 
                 if [[ -f "$dest" ]]; then
                     if cmp -s "$dest" "$src" 2>/dev/null; then

@@ -1395,8 +1395,8 @@ act_run_native_build() {
         _log_ok "Build completed on $host in ${duration}s"
         status="success"
 
-        # Use run_id if available to group artifacts
-        local artifact_dir="$ACT_ARTIFACTS_DIR/${run_id:-build-$tool_name-$(date +%s)}"
+        # Use run_id if available to group artifacts; isolate per-target to avoid name collisions
+        local artifact_dir="$ACT_ARTIFACTS_DIR/${run_id:-build-$tool_name-$(date +%s)}/${platform//\//-}"
         mkdir -p "$artifact_dir"
 
         # Small delay to ensure files are fully flushed on remote
